title: tephrosia alba
intro: Tephrosia alba, also known as White Horsefly Bush, is a perennial shrub native to South Africa, characterized by its white flowers and small, white or yellowish seed pods. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which have been attributed to the presence of various alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Tephrosia alba include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Tephrosia alba include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using this plant medicinally.
